title: Wallet: alloc_fresh_address() — fix diversifier advance race
description:
Fix a correctness bug: next_diversifier() advances by 1 but find_address(start) may land at start+k (k up to 255). Consecutive calls can issue the same subaddress.

What: add alloc_fresh_address(dfvk, store, account) that atomically allocates a unique subaddress.

Algorithm:
1. Add pub async fn alloc_fresh_address(dfvk: &SaplingDiversifiableFvk, store: &WalletStore, account: u32) -> Result<(DiversifierIndex, PaymentAddress, String)> in wallet/src/address.rs. (Returns DiversifierIndex, PaymentAddress, and the UA-encoded address string.)
2. Inside: call store.next_diversifier(account) -> start.
3. Call dfvk.find_address(start) -> (actual_di, payment_addr). Returns Err if 256-attempt search fails (practically unreachable but must be typed).
4. If u128::from(actual_di) > start: call store.advance_diversifier_to(account, u128::from(actual_di) + 1).
5. Encode payment_addr as a bech32m Sapling-only UA string using zcash_address unified::Address (same pattern as sapling_address_as_testnet_ua in e2e tests, but for the configured network).
6. Return (actual_di, payment_addr, ua_string).

Defensive rules:
- Never return a cached address. Each call MUST call next_diversifier.
- advance_diversifier_to is monotone and idempotent if actual == start; safe to always call it.
- Log only the diversifier index and encoded address, never key material.
- Function must not be marked async on the address encoding steps — only the DB calls are async.

Tests:
- Call alloc_fresh_address twice; assert returned UA strings differ.
- Call with a seed where start diversifier is known-invalid (construct a DFVK where index 0 is invalid); assert the returned index is > 0 and the counter is advanced past it.
- Oracle: diversifier index uniqueness is self-evident; UA string format verified by zcash_address parsing.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-wallet passes; two consecutive calls produce distinct addresses
